title: Adaptive Typography & Responsive Expression
purpose: A shared foundation for how typography and selected visual design elements should scale with the space they live in, using container queries, container query units, clamp() and clear min/max boundaries.

source_references.title: MDN Web Docs - CSS container queries
source_references.role: Primary technical reference for container queries and container query units.

source_references.title: MDN Web Docs - clamp()
source_references.role: Primary technical reference for setting minimum, preferred and maximum values.

core_message: Typography and selected visual elements should respond to the space they live in, not only to fixed viewport breakpoints.
svenska_spel_message: Our typography should stay clear, balanced and expressive across surfaces. Large messages can scale with energy, while critical information must remain calm, readable and stable.
principle_tagline: Scale with context. Stay clear in every size.

focus_areas.name: clamp()
focus_areas.description: Use clamp() to define a safe minimum, a flexible preferred value and a maximum size. This creates controlled fluid scaling instead of hard jumps between breakpoints.
focus_areas.why_it_matters: It lets expressive typography grow with the layout while preventing text from becoming too small, too large or visually unstable.

focus_areas.name: container queries
focus_areas.description: Use container queries when components should respond to the space of their own container rather than the entire viewport.
focus_areas.why_it_matters: A card, hero area or module can be narrow or wide regardless of screen size. Container queries make reusable components more context-aware.

focus_areas.name: container query units
focus_areas.description: Use units such as cqi, cqb, cqmin and cqmax when typography or design elements should scale in relation to the container.
focus_areas.why_it_matters: This makes scaling more precise and modular than viewport units when the available space depends on component layout.

focus_areas.name: min/max boundaries
focus_areas.description: Always define clear lower and upper limits for adaptive sizes.
focus_areas.why_it_matters: Boundaries protect readability, hierarchy and layout stability.

focus_areas.name: typographic balance
focus_areas.description: Scale typography to support hierarchy and rhythm, not only visual impact.
focus_areas.why_it_matters: Large type can create expression, but it must still help the user understand the page.

focus_areas.name: readability
focus_areas.description: Critical UI text should remain calm, stable and readable across sizes.
focus_areas.why_it_matters: Information about money, limits, errors, verification and responsible gaming must never become cramped, oversized or hard to scan.

focus_areas.name: controlled scaling of text and visual objects
focus_areas.description: Apply the same adaptive logic to selected visual elements such as hero objects, illustrations or 3D cubes when they need to scale with available space.
focus_areas.why_it_matters: Brand expression can adapt to context without competing with content or interaction.

connected_design_principles.connection: Adaptive type should improve understanding, not become a visual trick.
Text must remain readable and usable across layout sizes and contexts.
Large expressive typography and visual elements can scale with energy while staying controlled.
Critical information about money, limits, errors and responsible gaming should remain stable and legible.

use_when: Hero typography should scale with its available space.
A component is reused in different widths or layout contexts.
A card heading needs to adapt to the card width.
An editorial or brand expression needs to stay balanced across surfaces.
A visual object such as a 3D cube, illustration or hero element should scale with its container.
Viewport breakpoints do not accurately describe the real space available.
A module needs to stay readable in both compact and spacious layouts.
be_careful_when: The scaling could make text harder to read.
Expressive typography risks overpowering critical information.
Similar components may end up with inconsistent type scales.
Translated strings or real content create unexpected wrapping.
The design relies on fluid scaling without clear minimum and maximum boundaries.
default_approach: Start with the content hierarchy and readability need.
Use container queries for components and modules.
Use clamp() to set safe minimum, preferred and maximum values.
Use cqi or cqmin when the size should relate to the container.
Keep critical UI text more stable than expressive display text.
Test with real content, translated strings and edge cases.
Document where adaptive scaling is allowed and why.

do: Protect readability with minimum and maximum values.
Use adaptive sizing to support hierarchy.
Let expressive typography feel alive without becoming unstable.
Keep critical messages calm, clear and readable.
Apply the same controlled scaling logic to selected visual objects when useful.
dont: Do not use adaptive scaling as a visual gimmick.
Do not let type become too small, too large or cramped.
Do not scale reusable UI typography directly from viewport width when container-aware scaling is the better fit.
Do not rely only on viewport breakpoints for reusable components.
Do not allow expressive elements to compete with critical content.
Do not create unpredictable type scales across similar modules.
Do not turn this into full typography token documentation.

container_setup.description: Define a component as a query container.

adaptive_card_heading.description: Scale a card heading based on the card's inline size.

named_container_hero.description: Create a named hero container and scale the hero word within it.

adaptive_visual_object.description: Scale a visual object, such as a 3D cube or brand element, with its available space.

critical_text_stability.description: Keep critical messages readable and more stable.

suggested_section_copy.title: Adaptive typography & responsive expression
suggested_section_copy.intro: Typography should respond to the space it lives in. With clamp(), container queries and container query units, we can scale type and selected visual elements in a controlled way — with clear minimum and maximum boundaries.
suggested_section_copy.body: This helps us keep hierarchy expressive, layouts balanced and critical information readable across products, modules and devices.
suggested_section_copy.short_note: Use adaptive sizing to create clarity, not decoration.

figma_make_instruction.instruction: Add this as a concise technical foundation, not a full typography token or component documentation page. Show it as a practical example of how design principles translate into implementation thinking.
must_include: container query units such as cqi, cqmin and cqmax
controlled scaling of both text and visual objects
avoid: Do not mention font-size-adjust as the main solution.
Do not make it a large technical documentation page.
Do not present adaptive scaling as purely decorative.
Do not remove the Svenska Spel context around clarity, trust and responsibility.

short_prompt_for_figma_make: Add the attached Adaptive Typography & Responsive Expression JSON to the Figma Make kit. This replaces the previous font-size-adjust direction. Focus on clamp(), container queries, container query units, min/max boundaries, typographic balance, readability and controlled scaling of both text and visual objects. Keep it concise and connect it to accessibility, clarity, balanced expression and trust.
